news.Title: 台股開盤慘跌千點原因曝光！高檔AI股太危險？「避險大戶今天不買台積電」資金全哄搶這檔低調權值股
news.Content: 美國與伊朗的緊張局勢未見緩和，直接衝擊全球金融市場的風險偏好，加上地緣衝突導致國際避險情緒蔓延，日股與韓股今（18）日開盤同步續挫，台股也難逃一劫，開盤重挫並一度大跌超過1,000點，然 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 受地緣政治緊張局勢影響，台股開盤重挫逾千點，但隨後跌幅收斂，市場資金湧入AI供應鏈，顯示對基本面重視度提升。
• 聯電因具備低本益比與穩健殖利率優勢，成為避險資金與投信的青睞標的，顯示市場偏好防禦性權值股。
• 電子通路族群如擎亞、增你強等股價上漲，暗示下游拉貨動能強勁，緩解市場對基本面轉弱的擔憂。
• 分析師建議觀察2至3天，確認大盤站穩5日線且國際局勢緩和後再行加碼，避免盲目搶反彈。
• 法人認為，儘管短期受通膨與地緣政治干擾，但半導體與AI產業基本面強勁，中長期多頭目標不變，急跌是買點。

news.Title: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) Supply Chain Finance Platform Selected by Glencore for $2.55B Program
news.Content: Fidelity National Information Services Inc. (NYSE: FIS ) is one of the best low priced technology stocks to buy according to hedge funds . On May 12, FIS announced that its Supply Chain Finance Platform, formerly known ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The FIS Supply Chain Finance Platform, hosted on Microsoft Azure, enables corporates to monetize receivables and unlock working capital.
• This transaction, backed by a consortium of six financial institutions, is one of the largest oil and gas trade receivables transactions executed.
• The platform provides technology infrastructure, operational support, and complex reporting for multi-jurisdictional transactions, offering real-time monitoring and transparency.

news.Title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Launches Compute Scale-Up Server 3250 for Critical Workloads
news.Content: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (NYSE: HPE ) is one of the best low priced technology stocks to buy according to hedge funds . On May 11, HPE announced the availability of the HPE Compute Scale-up Server 3250, a purpose-built server designed for ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The server is powered by Intel Xeon 6 processors and features a modular architecture scaling from 4 to 16 sockets, supporting up to 64 TB of DDR5 memory.
• It is engineered for high performance in applications like enterprise resource planning and emerging agentic AI workloads, with advanced security and resiliency features.
• The system is the first scale-up server validated by the SAP BW Edition HANA benchmark with at least 48 TB of memory, offering significant performance boosts.
news.Keywords: server technology, in-memory databases, critical workloads, AI hardware, enterprise solutions

• 建議避開高本益比、低殖利率、法人轉賣的高位階股，轉而布局低本益比、高殖利率的價值型股票。

news.Content: Microchip Technology Inc. (NASDAQ: MCHP ) is one of the best large cap stocks to buy under $100 . On May 7, Microchip reported FQ4 2026 net sales of $1.311 billion, up 35.1% year-over-year, with GAAP EPS of $0.21 and ...

news.Title: 台灣晶片優勢恐倒數18個月？美專家驚曝「1到2奈米差距」
news.Content: 台積電是台灣的護國神山和矽盾，在全球科技與地緣政治中扮演關鍵地位，但美國創投家帕里哈皮提亞在最新《All-In Podcast》節目中指出，18個月後台 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美國創投家認為，18個月後台灣可能因美國本土半導體產業成長及中美自主供應鏈建立，失去戰略重要性。
• 專家指出，美國距離建立完整本土高階晶片能力僅剩「1到2奈米」差距，一旦突破，對台灣產能依賴將大幅下降。
• 全球半導體供應鏈正朝「去集中化」發展，美國與大陸加速自主半導體產業，削弱台灣作為全球核心供應樞紐的重要性。
• 有評論者反駁此觀點，認為台灣的戰略重要性不僅限於晶片，批評此論調在戰略與道德上極度貧乏。
• 專家認為，華為投入巨資發展本土晶片，台積電美國新廠已開始生產，顯示去中心化趨勢。
news.Keywords: 半導體戰略,自主供應鏈,去集中化,技術差距,地緣政治風險

news.Title: 〈焦點股〉台積電擬出售持股 世界跌停深鎖 三家子公司同步重挫
news.Content: 台積電 (2330-TW)(TSM-US) 上周宣布計畫出售世界先進 (5347-TW) 約 8.1% 的持股，將資源專注在核心業務 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台積電計畫出售世界先進約8.1%持股，以專注核心業務，此舉導致世界先進股價跳空跌停。
• 此利空消息亦衝擊台積電集團子公司，包括創意、精材、采鈺股價全面重挫，精材打入跌停。
• 台積電此次出售世界先進持股約15.2萬張，持股比將從27.1%降至約19%，預計交易金額約241.45億至295.11億元。
• 若以176.5元為交易價格計算，台積電處分利益約達718.16億元。
• 儘管台積電強調不影響與世界先進的策略合作，市場反應仍劇烈，世界先進股價深鎖跌停。
news.Keywords: 股權處分,核心業務,集團子公司,財務投資,股價重挫

news.Title: 抄底王Q1加碼亞馬遜、半導體股 清倉航空股
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-18 11:54:51 黃智勤 發佈

有「華爾街抄底王」之稱的知名投資人泰珀(David Tepper)首季大舉加碼科技巨頭亞馬遜(Amazon)和叫車平台Ub ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 知名投資人泰珀（David Tepper）首季大舉加碼科技巨頭亞馬遜(Amazon)和叫車平台Uber。
• 泰珀也增持了Sandisk、美光(Micron)和台積電(2330)等半導體股，顯示對AI基建和記憶體市場的看好。
• 當季泰珀大舉減碼中概股，並將手上三檔航空股全數清倉，反映其對市場趨勢的判斷。
• 美伊戰爭爆發導致航空燃油飆漲，對航空業帶來挑戰，是泰珀清倉航空股的原因之一。
• 泰珀因在股市崩跌時進場抄底並獲豐厚報酬，被稱為「華爾街抄底王」，其持股變動被視為市場風向球。
news.Keywords: 價值投資,科技巨頭,半導體股,部位調整,市場風向球

news.Title: 台股資金潮退卻？投顧：量能仍在可控範圍
news.Content: 台北股市繼上週五下挫逾500點後，今天(18日)一開盤受鄰近亞洲股市開盤走跌影響，不到10分鐘就大跌逾千點，也讓外界質疑台股資金潮是否退卻？國內投 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股因鄰近亞洲股市走跌影響，開盤重挫逾千點，但投顧認為是乖離率過高修正，中長期基本面未變。
• 台股4月創下單月點數與漲幅歷史新高，5月以來持續走升，但技術面面臨修正壓力。
• 川習會實質利多有限，加上美股上週五表現弱勢，影響亞洲股市表現。
• 分析師預期，AI趨勢未變，長線趨勢仍可期，但短期內指數可能陷入震盪整理修正。
• 投顧認為，指數整理過程中量能稍縮在可控範圍，基本面未變，長線趨勢仍可期待；新台幣匯率走貶加大台股壓力。

news.Title: Atreides點名亞馬遜Trainium是黑馬，台積電保守擴產成AI防泡沫關鍵
news.Content: 【財訊快報／陳孟朔】外電報導，科技投資人、Atreides Management投資長貝克(Gavin Baker)在最新訪談中拋出多項挑戰市場共識的AI判斷，直指當前最被低估的AI晶片不是 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 科技投資人認為亞馬遜Trainium是當前最被低估的AI晶片，而非輝達或Google TPU。
• 台積電採取保守擴產策略，有助於全球科技市場避免網路泡沫式的過度投資。
• 台積電的擴產節奏遠低於輝達期望的倍數式擴張，此舉成為市場在狂熱中保留供給紀律的關鍵。
• AI晶片競爭將走向GPU、ASIC、雲端自研晶片並行，對博通、亞馬遜供應鏈等帶來重新定價機會。
• 太空數據中心可能在未來兩年內驗證商業可行性，並對地面數據中心電力與散熱供應商產生影響。
news.Keywords: AI晶片,擴產策略,供給紀律,產業煞車系統,太空數據中心

news.Title: This AI Stock Has Every Quality Warren Buffett Looks for in a Long-Term Hold
news.Content: As arguably the most well-known investor in stock market history, it makes sense that people tend to follow Warren Buffett's philosophies and advice when it comes to picking great stocks. No investor is perfect, but Buffett's sustained success over decades before his retirement is, to put it lightly, impressive. Buffett himself isn't the biggest fan of tech stocks, but there are a few artificial ingelligence (AI) stocks that fit the qualities that Buffett looks for in a business. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft (MSFT) exhibits qualities Warren Buffett looks for in a long-term investment, including a strong competitive moat derived from its enterprise software reach (Windows, Office 365, Azure).
• The company has a history of consistently returning value to shareholders, evidenced by 21 consecutive years of dividend increases and significant share buyback programs.
• Microsoft's ingrained presence in business operations creates high switching costs, contributing to its long-term stability and profitability.
• Despite not being a traditional dividend stock, its consistent dividend growth and substantial share repurchases are attractive to long-term investors.
news.Keywords: competitive moat, enterprise software, shareholder value, dividend growth, share buybacks

news.Title: Prediction: This Will Be the Next $5 Trillion Company
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) sits alone as the only company that's worth $5 trillion or more. Still, there's another company knocking on the door of this exclusive club: Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) . Alphabet isn't too far off and currently has a market cap of nearly $4.8 trillion, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOGL) is nearing a $5 trillion market capitalization, currently at nearly $4.8 trillion, positioning it as a potential next trillion-dollar company after Nvidia.
• The company has rapidly advanced in AI, becoming a leader with its generative AI models and seamless integration into Google Search, which saw a 19% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1.
• Google Cloud revenue grew by 63% in Q1, boosted by sales of Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) to external clients, establishing it as a key platform for AI application development.
• While Alphabet's stock is up over 25% year-to-date, its valuation is at a decade-high, suggesting capped near-term upside compared to some peers, especially until further AI division growth is realized.
news.Keywords: market capitalization, AI leadership, Google Cloud growth, valuation, revenue acceleration

news.Title: 5 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks That Have More Than Doubled This Year and Can Still Go Higher
news.Content: As the world embraces artificial intelligence (AI), and companies that provide development infrastructure are thriving, AI stocks are driving higher market gains. As a group, the industry is outperforming the market. One standard signal is the Nasdaq-100 , which features trending tech stocks and is beating the S&P 500 this year. Many exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that focus on AI are doing even better. Consider the Roundhill Generative AI and Technology ETF , the iShares U.S. Power and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Several AI stocks have more than doubled this year, outperforming the broader market, with companies like Bloom Energy, Sandisk, Lumentum, Micron Technology, and Intel showing significant gains.
• Bloom Energy reported a 130% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 and raised full-year guidance, turning operating cash flow positive.
• Sandisk's NAND flash memory products are in high demand for AI data storage, with Q3 FY2026 revenue up 251% year-over-year, driven by a 233% increase in data center revenue.
• Lumentum's optical products are crucial for high-speed data centers, with Q3 FY2026 revenue up 90% year-over-year, and the company reported a GAAP operating profit.
• Micron Technology's revenue increased 70% year-over-year in Q2 FY2026, with earnings per share rising significantly due to demand for memory chips in AI applications.
news.Keywords: AI stocks, revenue growth, data center demand, memory chips, infrastructure providers

news.Title: Another billionaire dumps mega-cap stock for AI bet
news.Content: Stanley Druckenmiller is one of the most-watched money managers on Wall Street , and his Duquesne Family Office Q1 2026 13F filing just landed. The headline number is brutal for Alphabet bulls. Duquesne sold all 385,000 shares of Alphabet ( GOOGL ) Class A stock during the quarter. That same stake had been aggressively built up just one quarter earlier , from roughly 102,000 shares to 385,000. Amazon got the same treatment. Duquesne cut its Amazon ( AMZN ) position from 737,940 shares down to just 9,539 shares, a 99% reduction. Where did the money go? Mostly into Broadcom ( ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Stanley Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office sold its entire stake in Alphabet (GOOGL) and significantly reduced its Amazon (AMZN) position in Q1 2026.
• The capital from these sales was largely redeployed into Broadcom (AVGO), a key AI custom silicon maker, with a new stake of 195,955 shares.
• Druckenmiller views Broadcom as a strategic AI chip investment, designed to create custom AI accelerators (ASICs) that are more cost-effective than GPUs for specific workloads.
• Broadcom reported Q1 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue of $8.4 billion, up 106% year-over-year, with guidance for $10.7 billion in AI sales for Q2.
• The exits from Alphabet and Amazon are interpreted as a rotation from crowded mega-cap AI trades into infrastructure plays like Broadcom, rather than a negative call on AI itself.
news.Keywords: portfolio rotation, AI infrastructure, custom silicon, mega-cap exits, strategic investment

news.Content: With whispers of a valuation approaching $2 trillion, SpaceX stands on the precipice of potentially the largest initial public offering in history. SpaceX must overcome regulatory hurdles and make significant technological leaps over the next few years to meet investors' sky-high expectations. Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an ...

• SpaceX's business segments include reusable rockets, Starlink global broadband, and a burgeoning focus on AI infrastructure, leveraging its network and launch capacity for orbital data centers.

news.Content: When Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) filed its first quarterly 13-F under new CEO Greg Abel late last week, one move stood out from the rest: In his first three months running the conglomerate, Abel boosted Berkshire's stake in Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) by 224%, lifting the position to nearly 58 million shares worth about $23 billion. news.AI_Summary:

• Alphabet reported a 22% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 to $109.9 billion, marking its fastest growth pace in over two years and the 11th consecutive quarter of double-digit growth.
• Operating income jumped 30% to $39.7 billion, with operating margins expanding to 36.1%, driven significantly by Google Cloud's 63% revenue growth to $20 billion and tripled operating income.

news.Title: Is Micron the Next Nvidia?
news.Content: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) is having a moment. The maker of memory chips just posted second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year over year. Gross margins have scaled up past 70%, a figure that would have seemed absurd for a memory company just three years ago. The stock trades at a forward P/E below 10, while the semiconductor industry median is around 30. If you squint, it ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) reported a significant Q2 FY2026 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year-over-year, with gross margins exceeding 70%.
• The company's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) is critical for AI accelerators, with capacity sold out through 2026 and shortages expected next year.
• Micron's return on equity is 40%, and its addressable market could reach $100 billion by 2028, positioning it as a compelling 'pick-and-shovel' play in the AI ecosystem.
• Unlike Nvidia, which designs AI accelerators and controls the software ecosystem, Micron is a component supplier vulnerable to price competition from peers like Samsung and SK Hynix.
• The stock trades at a forward P/E below 10, significantly lower than the semiconductor industry median of around 30, reflecting market concerns about the cyclical nature of the memory market and long-term pricing power.
news.Keywords: memory chips, high-bandwidth memory, AI supply chain, gross margin, valuation discount

news.Title: What Is the Cheapest "Magnificent Seven" Stock?

• Tesla (95x CFO) and Nvidia (56x CFO) are the most expensive, though Nvidia's strong growth is expected to reduce its multiple.
• Meta Platforms (13x CFO) appears to be the cheapest, trading at a historically low multiple, potentially offering a buying opportunity.

news.Keywords: Magnificent Seven, valuation metrics, operating cash flow, relative valuation, value investing

news.Title: The AI Stock With a 10-Year Head Start That Wall Street Still Hasn't Fully Priced In
news.Content: As the expenses of building and running artificial intelligence (AI) data centers continue to surge, having lower costs is a huge advantage. One of the best ways to achieve this is with custom chips called application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). ASICs for AI are hardwired chips developed for specific tasks. Not ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (GOOGL) has a significant advantage in AI due to its decade-long development of custom Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), which are more cost-effective and energy-efficient than competitors' GPUs.
• The company has optimized its hardware and software stack around its TPUs, leading to lower costs for training AI models and running inference.
• Alphabet's eighth-generation TPUs offer specialized variations for training (TPU 8t) and inference/agentic AI (TPU 8i), paired with custom Arm-based CPUs.
• The company is beginning to allow select customers to purchase TPUs directly, presenting a new growth avenue.
• Despite its AI leadership and custom silicon, Alphabet's stock is considered undervalued with a forward P/E of 28 on 2026 estimates.
news.Keywords: custom silicon, AI infrastructure, cost advantage, inference, training

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Where are Amazon’s headquarters? Seattle and beyond
news.Content: Jef Bezos and his wife, MacKenzie Scott, drove from New York City to Seattle to start Amazon.com in a rented garage. news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon's original headquarters are located in Seattle, Washington, spread across the Puget Sound area.
• The company established a second headquarters, known as HQ2 or Metropolitan Park, in Arlington, Virginia, to accommodate its growing workforce.
• The Seattle headquarters houses over 45,000 workers, while the Arlington location can accommodate up to 14,000 employees.
• The article details the physical attributes and amenities of both headquarters, including office buildings, retail spaces, and employee facilities.
news.Keywords: headquarters, expansion, corporate locations, workforce

news.Title: Arm Holdings CEO Rene Haas Has a Big Warning for Intel and AMD
news.Content: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) and Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) have been among the hottest chip stocks on the market, delivering phenomenal gains over the past year as the artificial intelligence (AI)-fueled demand for their products is leading to impressive growth in their businesses. While AMD stock has soared 300% over the past year, Intel shares have posted even bigger gains of 413% as of this writing. AMD has benefited from the lucrative contracts it has signed with hyperscalers and AI companies for its AI data center graphics ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings (ARM) CEO Rene Haas issued a warning to Intel and AMD, highlighting Arm's potential to reduce data center capital costs.
• Arm is shifting its business model to produce its own silicon, starting with the ARM AGI CPU targeting agentic AI and inference markets.
• The AGI CPU is projected to deliver more than double the initially anticipated revenue, with over $2 billion expected in the next couple of fiscal years.
• Arm-based chips are projected to capture 90% of custom CPUs in AI servers by 2029, driven by hyperscaler adoption.
• Meta Platforms is a key hyperscaler customer for Arm's AGI CPU, with potential annual revenue of $15 billion from this product by fiscal 2031.
news.Keywords: AI, data center, custom silicon, competitive threat, market share

news.Title: POET Stock Jumped on $500 Million Deal With Lumilens. Its Financials Have a Long Way to Go.
news.Content: Announcing a deal is mostly viewed as a positive for a company. Yet, when Poet Technologies (POET) revealed that it had signed a $500 million deal with photonics-focused company, Lumilens, there must have been some in the market who believed that there could be a correction in the stock. However, there was no repeat of the Marvell (MRVL) episode, and Poet shares ended yesterday's trading session up a whopping 43%. The partnership aims to combine Poet's expertise in developing wafer-level photonic integration platforms and Lumilens's optical chipsets and advanced manufacturing capabilities to build advanced optical engines for AI data ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Poet Technologies (POET) announced a $500 million deal with Lumilens to build advanced optical engines for AI data centers.
• The partnership combines Poet's photonic integration platforms with Lumilens's optical chipsets and manufacturing capabilities.
• Despite recent stock volatility due to tax status issues and a canceled Marvell order, Poet shares surged 43% on the Lumilens deal.
• Poet's Optical Interposer technology aims to reduce cost, size, and power consumption for optical interconnects in AI infrastructure.
• The company's stock is up 180% year-to-date, though it faces challenges including tax status and past order cancellations.
news.Keywords: AI data center, optical engines, photonic integration, strategic partnership, market volatility

news.Title: ARM's Powerful Ecosystem Advantage Drives Market Leadership
news.Content: Arm Holdings plc ARM has built a formidable competitive advantage through a deeply interconnected ecosystem that effectively unites software developers and hardware manufacturers. This powerful two-sided network has evolved into one of the semiconductor industry’s strongest and most durable competitive moats. ARM’s architecture has steadily become the preferred ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arm Holdings (ARM) possesses a strong competitive advantage through its interconnected ecosystem of software developers and hardware manufacturers.
• Its architecture is the preferred standard for device manufacturers due to broad OS compatibility and a mature software ecosystem.
• The company's intellectual property powers nearly every smartphone globally, creating a significant barrier to entry for competitors.
• Qualcomm (QCOM), a key partner, relies on ARM cores for its mobile processors, further strengthening ARM's market dominance.
• ARM trades at a high forward price-to-sales ratio (38.94x) compared to its industry (9.68x), with a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).
news.Keywords: ecosystem advantage, network effect, market leadership, intellectual property, semiconductor architecture

news.Title: How AI Nodes Boost Demand for Entegris Consumables
news.Content: Entegris, Inc. ENTG sits in the flow of artificial intelligence-driven node migration, where each new process step raises the penalty for contamination and pushes fabs toward tighter purity control. That dynamic can make consumables demand feel steadier because it tracks wafer starts and process complexity, not just equipment spending. With node transitions increasing “content per wafer,” Entegris is trying to capture more materials intensity in critical steps. The company’s positioning in filtration, selective etch, chemical mechanical planarization, and advanced deposition is central to that playbook.
